LAW AND MOTION 301, DEMURRER, SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. APPEARANCES: ANDREW VAN ORNUM, ESQ., APPEARED ON BEHALF OF PLAINTIFF AND DEMURRING PARTY FIREMAN'S FUND. ERIC B. SIMON, ESQ., APPEARED ON BEHALF OF DEFENDANT AND OPPOSING PARTY BDM CONSTRUCTION COMPANY. THE COURT SUSTAINED THE DEMURRER AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION TWO (BREACH OF CONTRACT), SEVEN (BREACH OF COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING), AND EIGHT (DELARATORY RELIEF.) TEN (10) DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE DEMURRER WAS OVERRULED AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION (NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION.) PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE:JAMES J. MCBRIDE REPORTER: ERIC THRONE, CSR # 7855

ADDED TO LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR Hearing To Address The Issue Of Whether Or Not The Order Appointing A Receiver Should Be Stayed Until 12-31-02
Off Calendar Oct-18-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kathleen Darmagnac, Esq. of DILLINGHAM & MURPHY 397-2700, for defendant BDM Construction Co., Inc. and moving party
Richard A. Bunn, Esq. of Wolkin & Timpane 982-9390, for plaintiff and opposing party
* A R G U E D and the Court GRANTED DEFENDANT BDM CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC.'s motion To Address The Issue Of Whether Or Not The Order Appointing A Receiver Should Be Stayed Until 12-31-02. The motion is granted on the condition that defendant will pay net rent to the plaintiff, and plaintiff is to pay all costs for the receiver and/or a neutral party to examine the books on a monthly basis. Prevailing party is to prepare an order. (AJR/301/md) Court Reporter Patricia M. Evezich, CSR 1193

DEMURRER
Off Calendar May-08-2002 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Van Ornum, Esq., WOKIN & TIMPANE, 415-982-9328, appeared on behalf of Plaintiff and Demurring Party Fireman's Fund. Eric B. Simon, Esq., DILLINGHAM & MURPHY, (415) 397-2700 appeared on behalf of defendant and opposing party BDM Construction Company.

After argument the Court sustained the demurrer as to causes of action Two (breach of contract), Seven (breach of covenant of good faith and fair dealing), and Eight (delaratory relief.) Ten (10) days leave to amend. The Demurrer was overruled as to the Third cause of action (Negligent Misrepresentation.) Prevailing party to prepare order. (JJM) Reporter: Eric Throne, CSR # 7855
